data.title: F5 EVP Thomas Dean Fountain Sells 1,110 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameFountain Thomas Dean
RelationshipEVP Global Services & Strategy, 10% Owner
TypeSell
Amount$263,058
SEC FilingForm 4

Thomas Dean Fountain, EVP Global Services & Strategy at F5 and a 10% owner, sold 1,110 shares of Common Stock on December 1, 2025, at a price of $236.99 per share, totaling $263,058. Following the transaction, Fountain directly owns 19,163 shares of F5. The sale was executed under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan established on June 13, 2025.

SEC Filing: F5, INC. [ FFIV ] - Form 4 - Dec. 03, 2025

data.title: Dave Inc. Executives Sell Shares to Cover Tax Obligations
data.description:
Reporter NameRelationshipTypeAmountSEC Filing
Wilk JasonChief Executive Officer, 10% OwnerSell$1,506,471Form 4
Beilman KyleCFO and COO and Secretary, 10% OwnerSell$712,176Form 4

Dave Inc. CEO Jason Wilk and CFO Kyle Beilman have recently sold shares of the company's Class A Common Stock to satisfy tax obligations related to vested Restricted Stock Units (RSUs).

Jason Wilk, CEO and 10% owner, sold 7,393 shares on December 2, 2025, at a price of $203.77 per share, totaling $1,506,471. Following this transaction, Wilk directly owns 210,461 shares and indirectly owns 47,882 shares through a trust.

Kyle Beilman, CFO, COO, Secretary, and 10% owner, sold 3,495 shares on the same date at the same price, totaling $712,176. After the sale, Beilman directly owns 150,556 shares of the company's Class A Common Stock.

data.title: Hillenbrand Raises Dividend by 1.1%
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Hillenbrand raised its quarterly dividend 1.1% to 22.75 cents a share from 22.5 cents.

The board approved the new payout, which is equal to 91 cents a year, the company said Wednesday. It represents an annual yield of 2.9% based on the stock's latest closing price of $31.84.

The dividend is payable on Dec. 31 to stockholders of record on Dec. 16, the industrial company said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Corn, wheat retreat after gains driven by Black Sea jitters
data.description:
Key points:
  • Corn and wheat prices fall as Black Sea tensions ease
  • Ample global wheat supplies add bearish sentiment
  • Soybeans decline amid uncertainty over Chinese demand

By Julie Ingwersen

U.S. corn futures fell more than 1% on Wednesday and wheat futures also dipped as fears subsided about a military escalation in the Black Sea grain export region, analysts said.

Soybeans declined on uncertainty about Chinese demand for U.S. supplies of the oilseed.

Chicago Board of Trade March corn futures (CH26) settled down 6-1/2 cents at $4.43-1/2 per bushel and March wheat (WH26) ended down 2-3/4 cents at $5.38-1/4 a bushel. CBOT January soybeans (SF26) finished down 9 cents at $11.15-3/4 a bushel.

Corn and wheat erased much of the previous day's advances, which brokers had attributed to Russian President Vladimir Putin's threat to cut off Ukraine's access to the Black Sea after drone attacks on Russia-linked vessels.

By Wednesday, following a five-hour Kremlin meeting between Putin and U.S. President Donald Trump's top envoys, fears of a threat to grain exports appeared to have receded, even though no agreement was reached to end the war in Ukraine.

"They are not telling us any details, but it seems like maybe Mr. Putin is not going to try to stop Ukraine from shipping grain. He was rattling his saber yesterday," said Tom Fritz, a partner with EFG Group in Chicago.

Ample global wheat supplies, bolstered by bumper crops being harvested in Argentina and Australia, added to bearish sentiment.

Algeria’s state grains agency bought around 810,000 to 900,000 metric tons of milling wheat in an international tender, European traders said. Algeria’s purchases are optional origin, but traders suspect some of the wheat could be sourced from the Black Sea region or Argentina.

Soybean futures fell as traders continued to monitor U.S. soy sales to China. U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told the New York Times DealBook summit that China was poised to complete its commitments under a U.S.-China trade agreement, including the purchase of 12 million metric tons of soybeans, which the Treasury chief said would be finished by the end of February 2026.

A White House fact sheet dated November 1, after Trump met with Chinese President Xi Jinping in South Korea, had said that China would buy the 12 million tons during the last two months of 2025.

"Mr. Bessent just put the whammy on the bean market," Fritz said. "He let it drop that they (China) don't have to fulfill it until the end of February. We were always under the impression that it was going to be by the end of December," Fritz said, adding that by February, Brazil will be starting to harvest what is projected to be a record-large soybean crop that could dominate global exports.

U.S. shipments to China are gaining momentum, with at least six bulk cargo vessels scheduled to load with soybeans at Gulf Coast terminals through mid-December, according to a shipping schedule seen by Reuters on Tuesday.

data.title: Jefferies upbeat on Australia's WiseTech Global after Investor Day; shares rise
data.description:
** Shares of Australia's WiseTech Global rise as much as 4.6% to A$75.93, their highest level since October 28

** Stock on track to record its second straight session of gains, if trend holds

** Brokerage Jefferies says WiseTech Investor Day signals stronger execution on key initiatives: new commercial model, Container Transport Optimisation (CTO) rollout, and e2open integration

** Jefferies keeps "buy" rating on the logistics management software provider at a price target of A$89.00

** WiseTech on Wednesday held its Investor Day, where it flagged its CTO was live and expanding, with total addressable market well ahead of earlier products

** Co also flagged e2open cost synergies were ahead of schedule

** Jefferies more positive on WTC's Investor Day than last year, citing clarity on pricing and product scope; expects ~7% average price uplift and long-term margin benefits

** 12 of 16 analysts rate the stock "buy" or higher, four "hold"; their median PT is A$115, according to data compiled by LSEG

** Stock has fallen 37.9% YTD, including session's moves

data.title: PLATTS: 904--Platts Renewable Penetration Indices - 03Dec25
Trustmark Approves $100M Share-Buyback Program
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Trustmark's board authorized a new stock-buyback program under which up to $100 million worth of shares may be acquired through the end of 2026.

The program will go into effect on Jan. 1, the financial-services company said Wednesday.

The new authorization will replace Trustmark's prior buyback program, which is set to expire on Dec. 31.

As of Oct. 31, the company had about 60 million shares outstanding, it said.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com

data.title: Muzero Acquisition Corp Files for IPO
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

Muzero Acquisition Corp has filed for an initial public offering.

The company said it will sell 17.5 million units at $10 apiece, according to a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission on Wednesday.

Each unit consists of one Class A share and one-half of one redeemable warrant to purchase one share at $11.50 apiece, the company said.

Muzero said it intends to apply to have its units listed on the Global Market tier of the Nasdaq. It did not disclose its intended ticker.

The special-purpose acquisition company granted underwriters a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 2.6 million units to cover over-allotments. BTIG is listed as the offering's underwriter.

The company additionally said that a sponsor and its underwriter have committed to purchase an aggregate of 447,500 private placement units at $10 per unit for an aggregate price of about $4.5 million. If the underwriters' over-allotment option is exercised in full, the sponsor could purchase 486,875 private placement units for an aggregate price of about $4.9 million.

The private placement is expected to close simultaneously with the closing of the IPO.

The company said it will receive gross proceeds of about $179.5 million from the offering, or about $206.1 million if the over-allotment option is exercised. Both figures include proceeds from the private placement.

The Cayman Islands blank-check company plans to focus on acquiring technology-enabled businesses across any industry, it said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: EQB to Buy PC Financial From Loblaw for About $573.5 Million
data.description:
By Stephen Nakrosis

EQB Inc. entered into an agreement to acquire President's Choice Bank, PC Financial Insurance Brokers and other affiliated entities from Loblaw.

The company said it would buy PC Financial for 1.15 times book value at closing, excluding certain excess capital, for consideration of about 800 million Canadian dollars (US$573.5 million), subject to adjustment.

EQB said it will issue 7.2 million common shares to one or more subsidiaries of Loblaw, and make up any remainder in cash. EQB said it plans to finance the cash consideration with current balance-sheet resources.

The company said PC Financial's products and services, such as the PC Mastercard portfolio, would be part of the deal. The acquisition is expected to expand EQB's total customer base to nearly 3.5 million and add C$5.8 billion in assets, the company said.

In connection with the deal, EQB will enter into along-term strategic relationship with Loblaw and become the exclusive financial partner for the PC Optimum loyalty program. Loblaw will receive board-nomination rights, registration rights and pre-emptive rights.

The deal is expected to close next year.

Write to Stephen Nakrosis at stephen.nakrosis@wsj.com

data.title: Blank-Check Company Hennessy Capital VIII Files for IPO
data.description:
By Katherine Hamilton

Special-purpose acquisition company Hennessy Capital Investment Corp. VIII filed for an initial public offering.

The company plans to offer 17.5 million units for $10 apiece. Each unit will consist of one Class A share and the right to receive one-fifteenth of a share upon the consummation of an initial business combination.

The company's chair and chief executive is Daniel Hennessy, who has overseen several SPACs, including ones that merged with the school bus maker Blue Bird Corp., logistics company TFI International and rail-infrastructure company NRC Group.

His son Thomas Hennessy is president and a director. He has helped oversee business combinations with the real estate company LatAm Logistic Properties and the homeowner software provider Porch Group.

The company is looking for an acquisition target that operates in the industrial, clean energy or metals and minerals industries with $500 million or more in enterprise value.

It plans to trade shares on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol HCIC.

Write to Katherine Hamilton at katherine.hamilton@wsj.com
